evals.name: investigation-board
evals.prompt: I'm debugging a memory leak in our Node.js API. The /api/users endpoint is leaking memory on every request. I found a suspicious closure in src/handlers/users.ts that captures the entire request object, and the heap snapshot shows growing EventEmitter listeners. Can you set up an investigation board on the canvas so I can see the full picture?
evals.expected_output: Creates multiple nodes (bug description, code file, heap findings, hypothesis) connected with edges, arranged in a tree layout. Uses appropriate node types (markdown for findings, file for source, status for investigation progress).

assertions.name: creates-multiple-nodes
assertions.description: Creates at least 3 nodes representing different aspects of the investigation

assertions.name: uses-edges
assertions.description: Connects nodes with labeled edges showing relationships between evidence

assertions.name: uses-tree-or-arrange
assertions.description: Arranges the nodes using tree layout or explicit positioning for readability

assertions.name: uses-appropriate-types
assertions.description: Uses file nodes for source code and markdown/status for findings rather than all markdown

evals.name: architecture-diagram
evals.prompt: We're building a microservices backend with these services: API Gateway (Express, port 3000), Auth Service (JWT + OAuth2, port 3001), User Service (CRUD, port 3002), PostgreSQL (port 5432), and Redis (caching, port 6379). The API Gateway routes to Auth and User services, both Auth and User read/write to PostgreSQL, and User Service uses Redis for caching. Can you visualize this architecture on the canvas?
evals.expected_output: Creates nodes for each service with relevant details, connects them with flow/depends-on edges with descriptive labels, groups related services, and arranges in a readable layout.

assertions.name: all-services-present
assertions.description: Creates nodes for all 5 services/datastores mentioned

assertions.name: correct-connections
assertions.description: Creates edges matching the described relationships (Gateway->Auth, Gateway->User, Auth->PG, User->PG, User->Redis)

assertions.name: edge-labels
assertions.description: Uses descriptive edge labels (not bare unlabeled edges)

assertions.name: layout-applied
assertions.description: Applies auto-arrange or uses explicit positioning for readable layout

evals.name: task-plan-tracking
evals.prompt: I need to implement a new payment feature. Here's the plan: 1) Define payment schema (done), 2) Build Stripe integration (in progress), 3) Create payment API routes (blocked by #2), 4) Add webhook handlers (blocked by #3), 5) Write integration tests (blocked by #4), 6) Update API docs (can start anytime). Show this on the canvas as a task board with dependencies.
evals.expected_output: Creates status nodes for each task with appropriate colors (green=done, yellow=in-progress, red=blocked, blue=ready), connects with depends-on edges, and arranges to show dependency flow.

assertions.name: six-task-nodes
assertions.description: Creates exactly 6 task nodes, one for each task

assertions.name: semantic-colors
assertions.description: Uses green for done, yellow for in-progress, red for blocked tasks

assertions.name: dependency-edges
assertions.description: Creates depends-on edges matching the described blocking relationships

assertions.name: status-node-type
assertions.description: Uses status node type (not markdown) for task items

evals.name: read-pinned-context
evals.prompt: I've pinned some nodes on the canvas that I want you to look at. Can you read what I've pinned and summarize what you think I'm focusing on?
evals.expected_output: Reads canvas://pinned-context resource, summarizes the pinned nodes' content, identifies spatial neighborhoods, and explains what the human appears to be focusing on based on the pinned context and nearby nodes.

assertions.name: reads-pinned-context
assertions.description: Reads the canvas://pinned-context MCP resource (not just canvas_query action:layout)

assertions.name: summarizes-content
assertions.description: Provides a meaningful summary of what the pinned nodes contain

assertions.name: interprets-intent
assertions.description: Makes an interpretation of what the human is focusing on based on pinned context

evals.name: code-exploration-files
evals.prompt: I'm trying to understand how the authentication flow works in this project. Can you put the relevant auth files on the canvas so I can see how they connect? The main files are src/auth/login.ts, src/auth/middleware.ts, src/auth/jwt.ts, and src/routes/auth.ts.
evals.expected_output: Creates file nodes for each mentioned file (content auto-loads), relies on code graph to auto-detect import dependencies, groups auth files together, and reads canvas://code-graph for dependency analysis.

assertions.name: uses-file-nodes
assertions.description: Creates file type nodes (not markdown with pasted code) for source files

assertions.name: all-files-added
assertions.description: Adds all 4 mentioned files to the canvas

assertions.name: groups-files
assertions.description: Groups the auth files together in a canvas group

evals.name: status-dashboard-updates
evals.prompt: I'm running a deployment pipeline. Create a dashboard on the canvas showing: Build (passing), Unit Tests (running), Integration Tests (queued), Deploy to Staging (queued). Then update it - unit tests just passed and integration tests are now running.
evals.expected_output: Creates status nodes with semantic colors, arranges as grid dashboard, then updates nodes in place using canvas_node action:update (not delete+recreate) to reflect new state.

assertions.name: initial-dashboard
assertions.description: Creates 4 status nodes with appropriate initial colors

assertions.name: updates-in-place
assertions.description: Uses canvas_node action:update to change status (not remove+add)

assertions.name: grid-layout
assertions.description: Arranges nodes in grid layout for dashboard view

assertions.name: color-transitions
assertions.description: Updates colors to reflect new state (unit tests -> green, integration -> yellow)

evals.name: snapshot-before-changes
evals.prompt: I want to reorganize the canvas to explore a different approach, but I don't want to lose what's there now. Can you save the current state, clear it, and set up a fresh workspace for the new approach? If it doesn't work out I want to go back.
evals.expected_output: Takes a named snapshot of current state, then clears the canvas. Mentions that canvas_restore can bring back the old state. Does NOT just clear without snapshotting.

assertions.name: takes-snapshot
assertions.description: Saves a snapshot with canvas_snapshot before clearing

assertions.name: clears-after-snapshot
assertions.description: Clears the canvas after (not before) saving the snapshot

assertions.name: mentions-restore
assertions.description: Tells the user how to restore (canvas_restore) if they want to go back

evals.name: workspace-identity-preflight
evals.prompt: Attach to the running PMX canvas and add a status node for our deploy. There's already a daemon on port 4313.
evals.expected_output: Before mutating, reads GET /health (or serve status) and confirms the returned workspace matches the intended workspace root; refuses to mutate a mismatched/stale listener and starts an isolated server on an explicit --port instead.

assertions.name: reads-health
assertions.description: Reads /health or serve status before any mutation

assertions.name: checks-workspace
assertions.description: Compares the returned workspace to the intended workspace root

assertions.name: handles-mismatch
assertions.description: On workspace mismatch or responsive+pidRunning:false, does NOT mutate; isolates via explicit --port and re-checks /health

evals.name: current-composite-routing
evals.prompt: Create a markdown note, a bar chart, and an Excalidraw diagram on the canvas.
evals.expected_output: Uses current composites: canvas_node {action:add} for markdown, canvas_render {action:add-graph} for the chart, canvas_app {action:diagram} for Excalidraw. Avoids deprecated single-purpose tools (canvas_add_node/canvas_add_diagram/canvas_build_web_artifact/canvas_open_mcp_app).

assertions.name: uses-canvas-node
assertions.description: Creates the markdown node via canvas_node {action:add}

assertions.name: uses-canvas-render
assertions.description: Creates the chart via canvas_render {action:add-graph}

assertions.name: uses-canvas-app
assertions.description: Creates the Excalidraw diagram via canvas_app {action:diagram}

assertions.name: avoids-deprecated
assertions.description: Does not call deprecated standalones (canvas_add_node/canvas_add_diagram/etc.)

evals.name: existing-board-extension
evals.prompt: Add two more findings to the investigation board that's already on the canvas.
evals.expected_output: Searches the existing board first, snapshots before extending, adds new nodes without duplicating existing titles, and validates the final layout for collisions.

assertions.name: searches-first
assertions.description: Searches existing nodes (canvas_query {action:search}) before adding

assertions.name: snapshots-before
assertions.description: Snapshots before extending the board

assertions.name: no-duplicates
assertions.description: Adds new nodes without duplicating existing node titles

assertions.name: validates-layout
assertions.description: Runs canvas_query {action:validate} (or arrange+validate) on the result

evals.name: context-pin-workflow
evals.prompt: Pin the auth design node so I (the agent) keep it in context, then confirm it's pinned.
evals.expected_output: Pins via a verified path (canvas_pin_nodes, the CLI pin, or browser/right-click 'Pin as context'), then reads canvas://pinned-context to confirm; does not claim a broken UI action succeeded.

assertions.name: pins-via-verified-path
assertions.description: Pins via canvas_pin_nodes / CLI pin / 'Pin as context' — not an unverified action

assertions.name: reads-pinned-context
assertions.description: Reads canvas://pinned-context (or canvas_ax_state) to confirm the pin

assertions.name: confirms-count
assertions.description: Confirms the pin is reflected (context count / pinned set) rather than assuming success

evals.name: status-node-cleanup
evals.prompt: Remove the obsolete 'Deploy: pending' status node from the board.
evals.expected_output: Removes the status node via canvas_node {action:remove} (status nodes are removable like any other type) and verifies removal; undo remains available.

assertions.name: removes-status-node
assertions.description: Removes the status node via canvas_node {action:remove}

assertions.name: verifies-removal
assertions.description: Verifies the node is gone from the layout

assertions.name: preserves-undo
assertions.description: Notes/relies on undo (canvas_history) remaining available

evals.name: ax-html-control-surface
evals.prompt: Build an AX control surface as an HTML node with a button that creates a work item and a button that steers me.
evals.expected_output: Creates an opted-in html node (axCapabilities.enabled) using the blessed recipe: awaits window.PMX_AX.emit, reflects live state via pmx-ax-update, avoids sandbox-blocked localStorage, and labels the steer button as queued for the next turn (not immediate wake).

assertions.name: opts-in-ax
assertions.description: Creates the html node with axCapabilities.enabled = true

assertions.name: awaits-emit
assertions.description: Uses window.PMX_AX.emit with await (not fire-and-forget)

assertions.name: sandbox-safe
assertions.description: Avoids localStorage/sessionStorage/cookies in the sandboxed iframe

assertions.name: labels-steer-queued
assertions.description: Labels steering as queued for the next turn, not immediate agent wake

evals.name: ax-delivery-lifecycle
evals.prompt: There are pending steering messages from the board. Pick them up and act on them.
evals.expected_output: Reads compact context (newest-first pendingSteering + totalPending/omittedPending) and/or claims via canvas_ax_delivery {action:claim}; acts, then marks delivered. Understands direct delivery stays FIFO oldest-first.

assertions.name: reads-newest-first
assertions.description: Uses compact context (newest-first + counts) or canvas_ax_delivery claim to find fresh steering

assertions.name: acts-then-marks
assertions.description: Acts on the steering then marks it delivered (canvas_ax_delivery {action:mark})

assertions.name: understands-fifo
assertions.description: Recognizes the direct delivery queue is FIFO oldest-first, distinct from compact context

evals.name: final-cleanup-and-validate
evals.prompt: We're done with the test board. Clean up the temporary nodes and leave the canvas as we found it.
evals.expected_output: Removes retry/test fixtures, runs canvas_query {action:validate} to confirm no accidental collisions/dangling edges, and restores the baseline snapshot.

assertions.name: removes-fixtures
assertions.description: Removes the temporary/retry fixture nodes

assertions.name: validates-final
assertions.description: Validates the final layout (canvas_query {action:validate})

assertions.name: restores-baseline
assertions.description: Restores the baseline snapshot to leave the canvas as found
